topic: One Option (Abnormal / Developmental / Health / Human Relationships)
subject: psychology

items.q: What does the dopamine hypothesis of schizophrenia state?
items.correct: Positive symptoms are linked to excess dopamine activity in mesolimbic pathways.
items.misconception: Schizophrenia is caused by too little dopamine.
items.random_wrong: Schizophrenia is caused by too much serotonin.
items.why: Students reverse the direction. The original hypothesis was HYPERdopaminergia. The revised version notes hypoactivity in prefrontal areas for negative symptoms, but excess in mesolimbic for positive.

items.q: In Milgram's study, what percentage of participants gave the maximum 450V shock?
items.why: Students remember 'most' obeyed and assume all did. Actually 65% went to 450V; 35% defied. This nuance matters because it shows obedience is strong but not absolute.
Students reverse IV and DV. The IV is what you CHANGE (input); the DV is what you MEASURE (outcome). Mnemonic: 'I change the I.'

items.q: The Robbers Cave study was conducted by:
items.why: Correct answer: Sherif. “Asch” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Buss (1989) surveyed how many cultures on mate preferences?
items.why: Correct answer: 37. “10” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Hazan and Shaver publish adult attachment styles?
items.why: The real figure is 1987 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
